entities.name: Hermes Trismegistus
entities.description: Legendary figure associated with Hermeticism, often depicted as the syncretic combination of Hermes and Thoth.

entities.name: Corpus Hermeticum
entities.description: A collection of ancient Greek philosophical texts that form the foundational scriptures of Hermeticism.

concepts.name: Hermeticism
concepts.description: An esoteric tradition focused on spiritual enlightenment and direct knowledge (gnosis) through divine revelation.

concepts.name: Esoteric Tradition
concepts.description: A branch of mysticism or philosophy intended for a select few, emphasizing hidden meanings.

concepts.name: Gnosis
concepts.description: The direct, personal experience of divine knowledge central to Hermetic thought.

concepts.name: Hermes Trismegistus
concepts.description: The godly sage or 'Thrice-Greatest Hermes', symbolizing the unity of wisdom and power.

concepts.name: Esoteric tradition
concepts.description: A spiritual or philosophical path focused on inner knowledge and hidden wisdom rather than public dogma.

summary: Hermeticism is an esoteric tradition centered on achieving spiritual enlightenment and divine *gnosis* through direct revelation. This philosophy is attributed to the legendary sage Hermes Trismegistus, a syncretic figure combining Egyptian and Greek wisdom. The foundational texts of this movement are compiled in the *Corpus Hermeticum*, which outlines an inner path distinct from public dogma. Ultimately, these writings emphasize hidden meanings and the unity of wisdom, positioning Hermeticism as a specialized branch of mysticism intended for spiritual initiates.
